responses.global_analysis:
1. JSON Match: The video consists of spoken dialogue followed by an instrumental introduction. It did not match the song lyrics in the reference JSON for this segment.
2. JSON Usage: Discarded reference lyrics during dialogue and the instrumental intro due to the 'AUDIO IS SUPREME' and 'ZERO GHOST SUBTITLES' rules. All spoken dialogue has been manually transcribed and translated.
3. Timing & Pacing: Timed dialogue strictly to voice onset. Incorporated visual text subtitles for on-screen group/member introductions during the vocal-less transition period.

subtitles.original: お疲れ様でした！
プロログお疲れ様！
おー！
ビーフ？
ピンクビーフ！ピンクビーフ！
はい、せーの、うー、プロログ！
イェーイ！
うー、アイムスゲヨ、キキララ、ビビって！
すげー！
すげよ！すげよ！
イェーイ！
Well, uh, when I chose to do these songs,
subtitles.english: Well, uh, when I chose to do these songs,

subtitles.original: I really wanted to bring in Prolog because I know they're all really good at dancing.
subtitles.english: I really wanted to bring in Prolog because I know they're all really good at dancing.

subtitles.original: Yeah, and performing! みんないつも上手だから、絶対プロログ誘おうと、完成度高いと思って。
subtitles.english: Yeah, and performing! Since everyone is so good, I knew I had to invite Prolog, thinking the quality would be high.

subtitles.original: でも、なんか困ったこととか、難しかったフリとか、なんかありましたか？
subtitles.english: But, did you have any troubles, or was there any difficult choreography?

subtitles.original: 全部！
全部難しかった！
え、でもカッコよかった！みんな、なんか揃ったの見た時に、めっちゃカッコいい！ってなりました。
subtitles.english: But it was so cool! When I saw you all in sync, I thought, "That's so cool!"

subtitles.original: 私も、なんかそのみんなで、どんどん出来上がって、完成度が高くなっていくうちに、
subtitles.english: Me too! As we practiced and the quality got higher and higher,

subtitles.original: もう幸せすぎて！
すごいカッコいいって言ってくれてて嬉しかった。
subtitles.english: I was so happy you said it was so cool.

subtitles.original: なんか、カノンって結構、交互のフリみたいな...
subtitles.english: Like, in Kanon, there's a lot of alternating choreography...

subtitles.original: そうそう、こういうのね！
そういうの、今まであんまりなかったから、キアラ先輩にね、呼んでもらって、それが出来て嬉しかったです！
subtitles.english: We didn't really have that kind of thing before, so I was happy Kiara-senpai invited us and we could do it!

subtitles.original: 嬉しかったー！
そうだね、なんかあの、順番で回るとか、結構多くて、大変でした。
subtitles.english: Right, like rotating in order, there was quite a lot of that, so it was hard.

subtitles.original: まあ、すいません、難しいフリして。あの、みなさんのコーナー？
subtitles.english: Well, sorry for the difficult choreography. Um, your corner?

subtitles.original: キュートのコーナーがあって、キュートっていうグループは、ダンスがマジでいつも難しいです。
subtitles.english: There was a Cute corner, and the group Cute's dance is always really difficult.

subtitles.original: 確かに！実力派。
ごめんね、でもみんなよく出来てた！
subtitles.english: Sorry about that, but everyone did so well!

subtitles.original: めっちゃ楽しかった！ありがとうございます。
subtitles.english: It was so much fun! Thank you so much.

subtitles.original: キュートと言えば、I want to know everybody's like, favorite Hello! Project song?
subtitles.english: Speaking of Cute, I want to know everybody's like, favorite Hello! Project song?

subtitles.original: ハロプロの好きな曲！知りたいけど、じゃあまず、すーちゃんありますか？
subtitles.english: Your favorite Hello! Project songs! I want to know, but first, Su-chan, do you have one?

subtitles.original: 「アモレ」！「アモレ」！
おー！アモレ！アモレ大好き！
おー、良い。結構最近の曲ですね！良いですね。
subtitles.english: Oh, nice. That's a pretty recent song! Nice.

subtitles.original: あと、にこちゃんは？
にこちゃん、「バカヤロー」！「バカヤロー...」大好きです！
大好き！良いよね！すごく良いよね！
subtitles.english: I love it! It's good, right! It's really good!

subtitles.original: 分かる！と、結構ヲタクと聞いて...
subtitles.english: I get it! I heard you're quite a big fan...

subtitles.original: そうです！えっと、そう、私の好きな曲は、モーニング娘。'14さんの「時空を超え 宇宙を超え」
subtitles.english: Yes! Um, yes, my favorite song is Morning Musume '14's "Toki wo Koe Sora wo Koe".

subtitles.original: あ、それやるやつ！名曲ですね。
subtitles.english: Oh, the one we're doing! It's a masterpiece.

subtitles.original: 「時空を超え 宇宙を超え」ってすごい、カッコいい、しっとりした曲。
subtitles.english: "Toki wo Koe Sora wo Koe" is a really cool, beautiful song.

subtitles.original: 良いですね！でも、私は天邪鬼（あまのじゃく）もすごく好きです。
subtitles.english: It's nice! But, I also really like "Amanojaku".

subtitles.original: 天邪鬼！天邪鬼は、去年にやってたんです。
天邪鬼大好きです！
なるほど、おー、詳しそうですね！ヲタクだね。
subtitles.english: I see, wow, you seem to know a lot! A real fan.

subtitles.original: はーちゃんはどの曲が？
私は、「ソラシド〜ねえねえ〜」が、初めて聴いた時にめっちゃ素敵な曲だなと思って、大好きです！
subtitles.english: For me, "Solasido ~Nee Nee~", when I first heard it, I thought it was such a wonderful song, and I love it!

subtitles.original: Buono!ですね。それも、Buono!大好きです。
subtitles.english: Buono! indeed. We all love Buono!.

subtitles.original: みんな大好きなBuono!ですね。
分かる！と、びびわんは？
はい！びびわんは、「チュッ！チュッ！チュッ！サマーパーティ」！
subtitles.english: Yes! Bibi-one is "Chu! Chu! Chu! Summer Party"!

subtitles.original: キアラ先輩に、チュッチュしてほしいです！チュッチュッチュッ...
subtitles.english: I want Kiara-senpai to blow me kisses! Chu chu chu...

subtitles.original: やめてー！
キアラ先輩、騙されないでください！騙されてないよ！
subtitles.english: Kiara-senpai, don't be fooled! I'm not fooled!

subtitles.original: 可愛い！
でも、この色んな曲名を聞くだけで、このライブ、ハロプロカバーソングライブ、何度も何度もやりたいですけど、
subtitles.english: But, just hearing all these song titles makes me want to do this Hello! Project cover song live over and over again,

subtitles.original: とりあえずは、あの、まだまだあります！
subtitles.english: but for now, um, there's still more to come!

subtitles.original: キュートの次に、他のパートも、他のグループもありますので、
subtitles.english: After Cute, there are other parts and other groups,

subtitles.original: 次のパートに行きましょう！プロログのみんな、ありがとう！
subtitles.english: so let's go to the next part! Thank you, everyone from Prolog!

subtitles.original: ありがとうございましたー！
バイバイ！
というわけで、Let's go to the next corner!
subtitles.english: With that, let's go to the next corner!

subtitles.original: イェーイ！
S/mileage
犬神ころね
兎田ぺこら
ワトソン・アメリア
S/mileage

responses.thoughts:
**Analysis and Transcription for Subtitles (00:00 - 05:00)**

Okay, here's my breakdown of the video segment. The goal is precise subtitles.

First, I identified the sections. I need subtitles from 00:00 to 05:00. I can break it down in chunks. The first, 00:00 to 04:05, is primarily spoken dialogue between VTubers, a mix of Japanese and English. I’ll need to transcribe that meticulously. I heard them discussing a group called "Prolog," celebrating something, and then moving into more general conversation. The main task is to get the dialogue correct, including any overlaps. I focused on accuracy from the very start. I’m thinking, let's nail this down, since they're excited to start.

I did, indeed, need to transcribe what Kiara was saying. I had to listen closely a few times, so to make certain about "Prolog get the path," or variations of that. I then picked out other points like "Pink beef!" and, later, translating "うー、アイムスゲヨ、キキララビビて！" to “I am Sugesyo, Kikirara Bibitte!” and other sentences of the same tenor, for proper subtitles.

Next, I focused on accuracy. I had to determine timings, since accuracy is critical in this type of work, and those were adjusted as I went. I noted the English segments as well, making sure not to lose any nuance.

After that, I needed to make sure I had the right song name, which could have been tricky. But it was definitely the "バカヤロー" by Berryz Kobo and not, as I initially thought, "バカヤロー AISHITERU." I then focused on the other song references, like "時空を超え 宇宙を超え," which needed to be translated, and the other songs, all in Japanese as well.

The second portion, from 04:05 to 04:23, is an instrumental segment with visual cues. There are screen graphics for a group, and members (S/mileage, Inugami Korone, Usada Pekora, and Watson Amelia). These are visual events, so I must subtitle them. I’ll precisely time each graphic appearing on the screen. It is important to remember the zero-ghost subtitles! There is zero spoken dialogue, and I must adhere to that rule.

The final section, 04:23 to 05:00 is instrumental, and this is where I must show restraint. There are no vocals; just instrumental music, with Kiara, Pekora, and Korone on stage, and I am to REMAIN SILENT, following the "ZERO GHOST SUBTITLES" rule. I did need to double-check that vocals didn't start early, and I verified that there were none, and was careful to remain silent during that portion.

So, in the end, it’s all about precision: transcribed dialogue, visual event subtitling, and, above all, adhering to the "ZERO GHOST SUBTITLES" rule during silent segments. This should be as accurate as possible!
